title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Type-System Trace: Transfer Evidence Plan
summary: A team calls every background task reliable pub/sub, promises exactly-once delivery, and reports publish calls instead of stakeholder, ownership, loss, duplicate, latency, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transfer evidence plan increment in messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Plan browser models, TypeScript compilation, contract tests, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, and production transfer gates.
Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Transfer Evidence Plan · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9871 in a regional incident workflow during one broker-node restart. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; keep credentials and connection URLs out of learner output; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan browser models, TypeScript compilation, contract tests, disposable RabbitMQ integration, partition, restart, load, and production transfer gates.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Transfer Evidence Plan evidence · Theory Transfer Evidence Plan
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-ts-transfer-evidence-plan-1dpp10" that uses case 6097 to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqplib or Node built-ins, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24, amqplib 2.0.1, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.
